marketPage.calcSub: Calculate custom maker fees, stamp taxes and final receipts
marketPage.dcaSub: Plan regular savings & project protection from cash depreciation
marketPage.dcaNote: By saving {currency} {amount} monthly for {months} months, gold locks in value. Your estimated nominal protection premium totals +{currency} {profit} over standard paper {currency} depreciation.
marketPage.guideSub: Proven practices to maximize yield and avoid merchant markups
marketPage.guide1Body: If you save, buy standard coins and bars. Maker fees are only 50-80 EGP per gram compared to 150-300 EGP for decorated jewelry.
marketPage.guide2Body: Top local bullion brands offer partial refund of maker fees (cashback) upon resell, provided the original packaging remains sealed.
marketPage.guide3Body: Insist on getting a detailed tax invoice highlighting total net weight, karat purity, maker fee separately, and stamp certificate.
marketPage.guide4Body: Never buy gold lacking the Egyptian hallmarking stamp. Unstamped metals carry zero resale guarantees and lose legal status.
marketPage.proPremiumSub: Local markup premium vs global spot prices
marketPage.premiumLowDesc: Premium is low. Local gold pricing is highly aligned with global spot, making it an excellent buying window.
marketPage.premiumFairDesc: Premium is moderate and sits within historical demand averages and market margins.
marketPage.premiumHighDesc: Premium is high due to local speculative demand or parallel dollar concerns. Exercise caution against local pricing bubbles.
marketPage.impliedRateSub: Dollar rate implicitly priced into Egyptian gold markets
marketPage.impliedRateDesc: This indicator represents the currency value gold merchants use. A wide divergence between the implied and official USD rates indicates devaluation expectations or tight dollar liquidity.
marketPage.correlationSub: Correlation strength of local gold to global spot
marketPage.correlationDescVeryStrong: The local market is fully integrated with global spot prices, and local changes closely match global movements with steady dealer margins.
marketPage.correlationDescStrong: The local market tracks spot gold trends closely, with standard markup premiums and minimal local supply distortions.
marketPage.correlationDescMod: The local market shows moderate independence from global trends due to temporary local demand shifts or domestic exchange rate dynamics.
marketPage.correlationDescWeak: Significant decoupling between local and global prices, primarily driven by strong local speculative bubbles or parallel currency spreads.
marketPage.correlationDesc: The Egyptian gold market tracks global spot prices with an 85% correlation. Local movements are heavily dictated by spot gold trends with minimal immediate parallel market shocks.
marketPage.cashbackNote: * Cashback: Reputable brands (like BTC) refund ~60% of the initial maker fee if resold with original packaging intact.
marketPage.techIndicatorsSub: Daily mathematical status models for strategic entry & exit
marketPage.disclaimerText: All pricing displays, simulators, DCA calculators, and indicators are constructed via mathematical estimations based on global gold rates and local markup variables. They are meant for informational purposes only. We cannot guarantee matching actual local jewelry retail prices at physical transaction times.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Comprehensive technical and financial analysis of the gold market — daily, weekly and monthly

day.explanation: Gold is trading above the main support level at 5,420 EGP. Positive momentum continues, supported by a weak US dollar and rising demand from Asian markets.

week.explanation: Weekly movements point to a trading range between 5,350 and 5,750 EGP. The US Federal Reserve's decisions this week will be the primary driver of the price.

month.explanation: Monthly indicators support a strong bullish trend with potential testing of the 5,900 EGP level. Persistent inflation and geopolitical instability support gold demand.

analytics.premiumZoneSafe: Fair and Safe pricing (low premium). Excellent entry point for investing.
analytics.premiumZoneWarning: Moderate pricing inflation. Recommended to buy in batches (DCA) rather than full liquidity.
analytics.premiumZoneDanger: High speculative premium with bubble risk. Caution is highly advised before buying.
analytics.lawsTitle: Four Economic Rules of Gold Investment
analytics.lawRatioDesc: When the ratio is high (>80), silver is historically underpriced compared to gold, presenting a good value.
analytics.lawDcaDesc: Buying gold in regular monthly segments averages out market volatility and avoids buying local peaks.
analytics.lawFeeDesc: Bullion bars and sealed coins have much lower making fees and offer cashback refunds when resold.
analytics.lawFedDesc: An inverse correlation exists; rate hikes increase bond yields and drag gold down, while cuts push it up.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Clear and concise answers about gold investment and live rates
faq.q1: How are gold prices updated on the site?
faq.a1: Prices are updated in real-time, pulling directly from global spot gold markets and local exchange rate metrics every 60 seconds automatically.
faq.q2: When is the best time to buy gold in Egypt?
faq.a2: Gold is a long-term wealth preservation tool. We recommend Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA)—buying fixed amounts monthly to average out market volatility and avoid entry timing risks.
faq.q3: What is the difference between gold bars and jewelry for saving?
faq.a3: For savings, gold bullion bars and coins are preferred due to significantly lower merchant making fees (typically 50-80 EGP/gram) compared to decorated jewelry, and they feature a cashback refund upon resell as long as the seal is intact.
faq.q4: How do US Federal Reserve decisions affect local gold prices?
faq.a4: Fed rate decisions inversely correlate with global gold prices; rate hikes increase bond yields and lower gold spot rates, driving down local currency gold prices depending on USD exchange rate fluctuations.
faq.q5: Are Sabika's AI signals and calculators guaranteed?
faq.a5: Our pricing matrix, simulators, and technical indicators are calibrated based on daily volatility support lines and historical performances. They serve as strategic guidance and do not represent professional financial advisory.
